name: Craig Handy
abstract: Craig Mitchell Handy (born September 25, 1962) is an American tenor saxophonist. Born in Oakland, California, he attended North Texas State University and following this played with Herbie Hancock, Head Hunters, Art Blakey, Wynton Marsalis, Roy Haynes, Abdullah Ibrahim, Elvin Jones, Joe Henderson, Betty Carter, John Hicks, George Adams, …

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Count Basie Revisited: 1936-1994

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Craig Handy was there, playing more modern, linear. So it was a good combination of guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: We had ... Craig Handy on saxophone ... So it was a good tour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Drummond
about_quotes.evidence: "The Excurision" has Lovano and Craig Handy and Mor Thiam... That recording is the most spiritual of all the recordings that I’ve had.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Drummond interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003
